name: edge_bevel_loop
description: Edge Bevel on a CLOSED LOOP of 4 edges (a cube face's own perimeter -- the closest 'support-loop-like' selection an unsubdivided cube can offer). Each of the 4 corners has exactly 2 selected edges meeting it (valence-2 in the selection): a continuous 90-degree-turning ring, no bare ends, no 3-way junction. Reference topology: 8v/6f -> 12v/10f, ALL 10 faces are quads (fv-dist {4:10}) -- each turn gets exactly ONE miter quad bridging the two adjacent chamfer strips, no pentagon/triangle needed (contrast with edge_bevel_corner.json, where a 3rd selected edge at the SAME kind of corner forces a triangle+3 quads instead of a plain miter quad). vibe3d's CURRENT bevelEdgesByMask explicitly refuses this input (its 'endpoint-disjoint' guard in mesh.d skips any edge whose endpoint touches another selected edge, so mesh.bevel currently returns status:error / no-op on this whole selection) -- this fixture freezes the TARGET geometry for task 0391's cap fix, not a regression lock on already-working behavior.
source: Frozen reference-editor capture via the private capture harness's edge_bevel op (task 0391), case edge_bevel_loop_top_w01.json: the +Y top face's own perimeter edges on a unit cube, width=0.1.

provenance.harness: private capture harness (reference-editor edge-bevel tool, driven headlessly, mode=inset)
provenance.notes: Target geometry for a currently-unsupported gesture (vibe3d's bevelEdgesByMask endpoint-disjoint guard skips connected selections) -- expect this fixture to FAIL until the cap fix lands; frozen now so the fix has a real target.
